queue.title: Add pruning-table boundary and tiny fixture
queue.prompt: After the checked two-phase coordinate set is complete, define the internal pruning-table boundary and add a deliberately tiny deterministic fixture for tests. Add compact table metadata, checked lookup behavior, a safe storage or gitignore policy for future generated artifacts, and a small generator or fixture path that proves solved and shallow-state lookups without committing full-size tables. Keep product solver defaults unchanged.
acceptance: pruning-table metadata records coordinate names, dimensions, generation parameters, phase role, and version information
lookup APIs distinguish missing entries, out-of-range indexes, mismatched metadata, and available distances without panics
a deterministic tiny fixture or generator path covers solved-state and shallow-state distances
tests cover successful lookups plus corrupted, mismatched, missing, and out-of-range fixture failures
large generated pruning-table locations are documented or gitignored
the product solver does not depend on generated pruning tables by default
existing validation, solve, WASM, web, and E2E product flows remain green
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Add deterministic pruning-table generation pipeline
queue.prompt: After the pruning-table lookup boundary and tiny fixture exist, add a deterministic generation and loading pipeline for pruning-table artifacts. Provide a CI-safe tiny generation profile exercised by tests, metadata-compatible loading through the checked lookup boundary, and a package command for tiny generation validation. Document or gitignore future full-size artifact locations, but do not commit full-size pruning tables or make product defaults require generated artifacts.
acceptance: generation profiles record coordinate names, dimensions, phase role, move set, generation limits, artifact format version, and deterministic seed or traversal rules
a CI-safe tiny profile deterministically generates pruning data from engine moves and writes metadata compatible with the lookup boundary
loading distinguishes missing artifacts, corrupted data, mismatched metadata, out-of-range indexes, and available distances without panics
npm run pruning:test validates tiny generation, loading, solved-state distance, and shallow-state distances
future full-size pruning-table artifact locations are documented or gitignored
product solver defaults and web behavior do not depend on generated pruning artifacts
existing validation, solve, WASM, web, and E2E product flows remain green
no full-size generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Integrate an explicit two-phase solver baseline
queue.prompt: Integrate the completed coordinate helpers, pruning-table lookup boundary, and deterministic generation/loading pipeline into a cube-engine two-phase baseline behind explicit SolverConfig selection. The baseline may remain limited while only tiny committed fixtures or locally generated artifacts exist, but it must return replay-verified moves, preserve distinct invalid-input and not-found-within-limits errors, and keep the existing bounded solver as the default product path. Do not claim optimality or a 20-move guarantee.
acceptance: two-phase search can be selected explicitly through cube-engine SolverConfig without changing the default bounded solver path
solved and known shallow cubie and facelet states return replay-verified solutions through the selected two-phase path using committed tiny fixtures or generated tiny artifacts
selected two-phase behavior is documented or encoded as a limited baseline while full generated tables may be absent
missing or unavailable generated pruning artifacts produce structured limit or availability behavior instead of panics
invalid input and not-found-within-limits errors remain distinct and include configured limits plus explored nodes where applicable
default validation, solve, WASM, web, and E2E behavior remains unchanged and green
public API names, errors, and tests avoid unsupported optimality or 20-move guarantees
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Add solver quality benchmarks and reporting
queue.prompt: After an explicit two-phase baseline exists, add deterministic solver quality fixtures and a lightweight benchmark or report. Measure solved states, shallow scrambles, and selected harder known valid states by solver selection, configured limits, solution length, explored nodes, elapsed time, and limit outcome. Keep results honest and reproducible without promising optimality or a 20-move guarantee.
acceptance: quality fixtures include solved, shallow, and selected harder valid states
reporting captures solver selection, configured limits, solution length, explored nodes, elapsed time, success, and not-found-within-limits outcomes
npm run solver:bench or an equivalent documented command runs the lightweight deterministic report
benchmark output is deterministic enough for regression comparison or clearly documented as local measurement output
large benchmark artifacts are gitignored or not produced
web and E2E product behavior remains green
reports avoid unsupported optimality and 20-move guarantee claims
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Expose solver strategy selection through product boundaries
queue.prompt: After the explicit two-phase baseline and solver quality report exist, expose honest solver strategy selection through the WASM and frontend boundaries. The web UI can keep the bounded solver as the default while optionally selecting the two-phase baseline, showing configured limits, solver mode, metrics, unavailable-artifact or limit failures, and replay-backed playback without duplicating cube logic or claiming optimality.
acceptance: WASM solve accepts a documented solver strategy or maps unsupported and unavailable strategies to JavaScript-friendly structured errors
frontend boundary represents solver mode, configured limits, success metrics, invalid input, not-found-within-limits, and unavailable-artifact outcomes distinctly
web UI keeps the existing bounded solver as the default and labels the two-phase path honestly as limited or experimental while full tables are absent
successful solutions from any exposed strategy are replay-verified before success and playback continues to use engine-produced states
Playwright covers the default product flow plus the exposed solver strategy selection or its honest unavailable/limit outcome
frontend code does not duplicate cube validation, move application, pruning lookup, or solver logic
UI text avoids unsupported optimality or 20-move guarantee claims
no large generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or model artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Define dataset schema and deterministic generator
queue.prompt: After the web product flow is covered by E2E, solver strategy selection is wired through product boundaries, and the classical solver baseline has quality reporting, define a versioned dataset schema and deterministic small dataset generator. Generate states through cube-engine, validate them before writing, include explicit label semantics, deduplicate states, define train/validation/test split rules, and keep large generated data out of git.
acceptance: dataset records include schema version, serialized state, generation seed or source, and validation metadata
labels distinguish generated scramble_depth from verified_solution_length and avoid calling generated depth optimal unless proven
best_move is present only when derived from a verified solution or documented reversible scramble path
generator output is deterministic for a fixed seed and deduplicates states
small fixture datasets are committed for tests while large dataset paths are gitignored
dataset generation reuses cube-engine validation and does not duplicate solver logic in scripts
WASM, web, and E2E product behavior remains green
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

queue.title: Train a minimal value-network baseline
queue.prompt: After dataset generation exists, implement the first ML baseline as a small Python/PyTorch MLP value network that consumes the versioned dataset fixture and predicts distance or distance bucket from cubie-derived features. Keep training and evaluation reproducible on a tiny fixture and do not integrate the model into the product solver yet.
acceptance: ML code consumes the dataset schema instead of parsing frontend color arrays as the source of truth
input features are derived from cubie representation or documented serialized engine state
the baseline model is an MLP value network, not a Transformer or reinforcement-learning agent
training and evaluation run on a tiny fixture with deterministic seed configuration
metrics include loss plus distance or bucket accuracy reporting
no large datasets or model checkpoints are committed
the product solver default path remains deterministic and unchanged
WASM, web, and E2E product behavior remains green

queue.title: Evaluate value network against solver baseline
queue.prompt: Evaluate the value-network baseline against classical heuristics and deterministic solver benchmark fixtures. Report error by depth or bucket, inference time, and offline move-ordering impact on explored nodes. Keep the model out of pruning and product defaults unless later tasks prove safe integration.
acceptance: evaluation reports error or bucket accuracy by generated depth range
evaluation reports inference time on fixture data
offline comparison includes classical heuristic or solver benchmark baselines
move-ordering analysis is offline and does not alter product solver behavior
results clearly state that learned estimates are not admissible unless proven
no large reports, datasets, or checkpoints are committed
WASM, web, and E2E product behavior remains green

queue.title: Experiment with ML-guided move ordering
queue.prompt: Add the first hybrid-search experiment by using learned scores only for move ordering, behind explicit configuration and with deterministic classical fallback. Preserve validation, limit reporting, and replay verification. Compare explored nodes, time, and solution length against the classical baseline before considering any learned pruning.
acceptance: hybrid mode is opt-in and the classical solver remains the default fallback
learned scores affect move ordering only and are not used as an admissible pruning bound
returned solutions are replay-verified before success
invalid input and not-found-within-limits errors remain distinct
benchmarks compare explored nodes, elapsed time, and solution length against the classical baseline
documentation or result text states limitations and avoids optimality or 20-move guarantees
WASM, web, and E2E product behavior remain green with hybrid mode opt-in
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings

history.title: Build deterministic cubie engine foundation
history.prompt: Implement the pure Rust cube engine foundation with cubie state, moves, notation, scrambles, validation, solved-state detection, and deterministic tests.
acceptance: cubie representation tracks corner and edge permutation plus orientation
moves and inverse move behavior mutate cubie state deterministically
notation, algorithms, and scrambles are parsed or rendered by the engine
cube validation rejects duplicate, missing, orientation, permutation, and parity-invalid states
solved-state detection exists for engine states
deterministic Rust tests cover public cube behavior

history.title: Accept and validate Kociemba facelet strings
history.prompt: Implement user-facing 54-character Kociemba facelet input, facelet-to-cubie conversion, cubie-to-facelet rendering, and structured validation errors without replacing the engine cubie representation.
acceptance: 54-character facelet strings parse with U, R, F, D, L, and B symbols
length, symbols, counts, and fixed centers are validated before solving
corners and edges decode from facelets into CubieState
CubieState renders back to facelets for round-trip and playback
syntax, center, decoding, and cubie validation errors remain distinct
valid facelet states convert to cubies and impossible states are rejected before solve

history.title: Provide bounded verified solver entry points
history.prompt: Expose a correct bounded solver API for cubie and facelet states with explicit limits, metrics, structured errors, and replay verification before returning success.
acceptance: IDA* search supports maximum depth and optional node budget
simple admissible heuristics are available for bounded search paths
public config, result, metrics, and error types are exported
CubieState and facelet solving use the same engine solver path
returned solutions are replay-verified before success
invalid input and not-found-within-limits errors remain distinct with metrics
solved states and shallow scrambles return verified solutions

history.title: Expose validation, solve, and playback through WASM
history.prompt: Expose cube-engine validation, solve, solved-facelet retrieval, playback, and final solved verification to JavaScript through wasm-bindgen with JavaScript-friendly results.
acceptance: crates/wasm exists with wasm-bindgen exports
WASM validation delegates to cube-engine facelet parsing, conversion, and validation
WASM solve accepts explicit limits and returns notation, length, metrics, invalid-input errors, and limit failures
WASM playback returns engine-produced facelet states and final solved verification
JavaScript-friendly statuses distinguish success, validation errors, notation errors, and limit failures
WASM wrapper tests cover success and primary failure paths

history.title: Build the React web solve and playback flow
history.prompt: Build a Vite React/TypeScript web app that loads the generated WASM package through a boundary, accepts facelet input, validates states, solves with visible limits, displays notation and metrics, and supports engine-backed playback.
acceptance: apps/web contains a buildable React/TypeScript application
WASM initialization is isolated from React components in a frontend boundary
UI accepts 54-character facelet input with validation messages
solve controls expose max_depth and max_nodes limits
solution notation, length, explored nodes, and configured limits are displayed
playback or step-through uses engine-produced states and final solved verification
layout includes desktop and mobile responsive behavior
frontend does not duplicate cube validation, move application, or solver logic

history.title: Cover solved, shallow, invalid, and playback product flows
history.prompt: Add Playwright coverage for the product flow so automated tests submit known states, request solutions, step playback, and verify visible engine-backed solved indicators without duplicating cube logic in tests.
acceptance: npm run test:e2e is available
E2E setup builds or documents the required WASM build before app build
tests submit a solved state and verify a no-op solution
tests submit a shallow scrambled state and verify displayed moves plus playback controls
tests step to the final playback state and verify the visible solved indicator
tests verify invalid-state UI errors
Playwright tests do not implement cube replay or solved checks outside the app
E2E artifacts are gitignored

history.title: Add initial checked two-phase coordinate helpers
history.prompt: Add initial checked two-phase coordinate helpers for corner orientation, edge orientation, UD-slice edge-combination membership, and corner permutation without changing product solver selection.
acceptance: corner-orientation coordinate exposes an explicit 2187-state range and checked reconstruction
edge-orientation coordinate exposes an explicit 2048-state range and checked reconstruction
UD-slice edge-combination coordinate exposes an explicit 495-state range over FR, FL, BL, and BR membership
corner-permutation coordinate exposes an explicit 40320-state range and checked unranking
solved-state and shallow-move deterministic tests cover round-trips and invalid indexes
helpers return structured errors instead of panicking for public invalid inputs
product solver defaults and web flow remain unchanged

history.title: Complete checked two-phase permutation coordinates
history.prompt: Complete the remaining checked two-phase permutation coordinate helpers now that orientation coordinates, UD-slice edge-combination coordinates, and corner-permutation coordinates exist. Add slice-edge permutation ranking/unranking for FR, FL, BL, and BR in slice positions with an explicit 4! range, plus U/D-edge permutation ranking/unranking for UR, UF, UL, UB, DR, DF, DL, and DB in U/D-layer positions with an explicit 8! range. Keep helpers data-oriented with structured errors and no standalone full-state validity claims. Do not add pruning tables, transition tables, search integration, frontend/WASM changes, datasets, or ML in this step.
acceptance: slice-edge permutation coordinate helpers have an explicit 24-state range and use the engine slice-edge set FR, FL, BL, and BR
U/D-edge permutation coordinate helpers have an explicit 40320-state range and use the engine U/D edge set UR, UF, UL, UB, DR, DF, DL, and DB
solved permutation fixtures map to coordinate 0 and round-trip through checked unranking
at least one deterministic phase-two-preserving moved fixture for each coordinate has a non-solved coordinate and round-trips
duplicate, missing, wrong-set, wrong-position, and out-of-range cases return structured errors instead of panicking in public helpers
unranking returns permutation data only and avoids claiming full cube validity without paired parity checks
coordinate code remains confined to cube-engine helpers and independent from frontend, WASM, datasets, and ML code
existing product solve, WASM, web, and E2E flows remain green
no generated pruning tables, datasets, benchmark reports, or large artifacts are committed
verification: if [ -f "$HOME/.cargo/env" ]; then . "$HOME/.cargo/env"; fi; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings
